example_summary: The first enantioselective total synthesis of (–)-denticulatin A was accomplished by W. Oppolzer. The key step in their approach was based on enantiotopic group differentiation in a meso dialdehyde by an aldol reaction. In the aldol reaction they utilized a bornanesultam chiral auxiliary. The enolization of N-propionylbornane-10,2-sultam provided the (Z)-borylenolate derivative, which underwent an aldol reaction with the meso dialdehyde to afford the product with high yield and enantiopurity.

extracts.transformation_text: enantiotopic group differentiation in a meso dialdehyde by an aldol reaction
extracts.products_text: aldol product with high yield and enantiopurity
extracts.reagents_text: i. Et2BOTf, i-Pr2NEt; ii. meso dialdehyde
extracts.notes_text: Utilized a bornanesultam chiral auxiliary. The enolization of N-propionylbornane-10,2-sultam provided the (Z)-borylenolate derivative, which underwent an aldol reaction with the meso dialdehyde.
